sourceMovie.title: Emily the Criminal
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Emily the Criminal (2022) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. John Patton Ford's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Tango & Cash
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Emily the Criminal clicked for you, Tango & Cash (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Kiss the Girls

faq.question: What should I watch after Emily the Criminal?
faq.answer: Try Tango & Cash, Kiss the Girls, Double Impact—each shares DNA with Emily the Criminal while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Emily the Criminal?
faq.answer: Emily the Criminal (2022) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Emily the Criminal worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
